title: Deprecation Header — Schema Lifecycle Marker
description: Schema-bound deprecation marker for portfolio schemas. Encodes a structured deprecation block that any schema can carry to declare its lifecycle state. Per memory #19 (no-md-terminus): schema lifecycle status must be schema-bound, not documented in a separate prose file. Per RULE-CI-DOCUMENTED-OVERRIDE-NOT-COMPLIANCE-1 the schema applies the forbidden-audit-paint-names constraint via allOf. The deprecation block is the LEGITIMATE structural marker for a schema's lifecycle position — distinct from audit-paint patterns that try to document violations into compliance. Pilot integration: schemas/_archive/venture-summary.schema.json carries the inaugural deprecation header (d-2026-06-02-deprecation-header-convention). Convention: schemas carrying a deprecation block declare status as one of active|deprecated|retired; deprecated and retired schemas remain accessible at canonical URL for backward reference but are excluded from active CI gating. Schemas at active status may omit the block entirely.

deprecation.description: The deprecation marker block. When present, declares the schema's lifecycle position. When absent, schema is implicitly active.

status.description: active: in normal use. deprecated: still served at canonical URL but not for new instances. retired: served only for archaeology; new instances rejected.

deprecatedDate.description: ISO 8601 date when deprecation status was first set. Should match the changelog entry that recorded the archival or deprecation decision.

supersededBy.description: URL or schema $id of the canonical replacement, if one exists. Null when deprecation is terminal with no replacement.

rationale.description: Why the schema was deprecated. Cites the decision record (DEC ID, FU ID, or changelog entry) that authorized the deprecation.

archiveLocation.description: Repo-relative path where the deprecated schema file lives. Typically schemas/_archive/<name>.schema.json after archival deliverables execute.
